wines.name: Taylor's Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged around two years in seasoned oak vats before bottling, then matures in bottle

taste.summary: Taylor's declared Vintage is the firm, structured benchmark of the style: dense black-fruit concentration over grippy tannins built for decades in bottle.

pairings.why: Classic Vintage Port and blue cheese: the salty crystalline cheese is cut by the wine's sweet black-fruit core.
Bitter cocoa meets the wine's cassis and liquorice for a rich after-dinner match.
The firm tannins frame gamey meat the way the wine ages around its fruit.

history.summary: Taylor's has declared Vintage Port from its Douro quintas (notably Vargellas) since the 17th century, building a reputation for the firmest, longest-lived classic style.

milestones.event: Earliest Taylor's records of Port shipping
Quinta de Vargellas acquired, becoming the backbone of the Vintage

wines.description: Taylor's declared Vintage Port, the firm and long-lived classic benchmark from the Fladgate house, built around the Vargellas estate.

wines.name: Taylor's Late Bottled Vintage
wines.oak_regime: Aged four to six years in wood before bottling, ready to drink on release

taste.summary: A rich, approachable LBV with deep black-fruit and a smooth cocoa-spiced finish, made to drink without decanting.

pairings.why: The wood-aged sweetness balances a sharp aged cheese.
Cocoa-on-cocoa richness with the wine's plummy fruit.
The custard tart's caramel echoes the LBV's wood spice.

history.summary: Taylor's pioneered the modern Late Bottled Vintage category, releasing the first commercial LBV with a date on the label.

milestones.event: Taylor's launches the first branded Late Bottled Vintage

wines.description: Taylor's Late Bottled Vintage, the house that pioneered the category, a rich ready-to-drink black-fruited Port. Best enjoyed 0 to 5 from release.

wines.name: Taylor's 10 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A nutty, mellow ten-year tawny with dried-fruit sweetness and a clean cask-aged finish.

pairings.why: Toasted nuts mirror the cask-aged walnut character.
Caramelised custard meets the tawny's honeyed nuttiness.
The tawny's dried fruit lifts the rich sheep's cheese.

history.summary: Aged tawnies are a Taylor's speciality, blended from casks to a consistent ten-year average age profile.

wines.description: Taylor's 10 Year Old Tawny, a nutty mellow cask-aged Port with dried-fruit sweetness. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Taylor's 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: Taylor's signature aged tawny: a fine balance of dried-fruit concentration, mellow nuttiness and a long spiced finish, widely cited as the benchmark twenty-year style.

pairings.why: Concentrated dried fruit and nuts echo the wood-aged register.
The tawny's butterscotch core meets a salted-caramel sweetness.
Marmelada and aged cheese frame the wine's candied citrus.

history.summary: The 20 Year Old is the heart of Taylor's age-dated tawny range, long regarded as a reference point for the category.

wines.description: Taylor's 20 Year Old Tawny, the benchmark aged tawny balancing dried fruit, nuttiness and a long spiced finish. Best enjoyed 0 to 5 from release.

wines.name: Taylor's 30 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average thirty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A deeply concentrated thirty-year tawny with intense dried fruit, toffee and a complex spiced wood character.

pairings.why: Dense dried-fruit sweetness mirrors the wine.
Roasted coffee notes link the wine and the dessert.
The wine's mellow intensity carries a strongly flavoured cheese.

history.summary: The 30 Year Old reflects decades of cask ageing in the Taylor's lodges in Gaia.

wines.description: Taylor's 30 Year Old Tawny, a deeply concentrated cask-aged Port with toffee, dried fruit and spice. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Taylor's 40 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average forty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: An intensely complex forty-year tawny, the dried fruit reduced to concentrated marmalade with layers of nut, spice and aged wood.

pairings.why: The wine's concentration stands up to the strongest cheeses.
Complex enough to be a dessert in itself.

history.summary: The 40 Year Old crowns Taylor's age-dated tawny range, drawn from the house's oldest cask reserves.

wines.description: Taylor's 40 Year Old Tawny, the intensely complex crown of the house's age-dated tawny range. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Taylor's Chip Dry White Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in seasoned oak vats, made in a drier white style

taste.summary: The first dry white Port, nutty and lightly honeyed, classically served chilled or as a Port and tonic aperitif.

pairings.why: The dry nutty profile is built for an aperitif spread.
Chip Dry with tonic and mint is the house aperitif serve.
Crisp dryness cuts the oily fish.

history.summary: Taylor's created Chip Dry in 1934 as the first dry white Port, establishing the aperitif style.

milestones.event: Chip Dry launched as the first dry white Port

wines.description: Taylor's Chip Dry, the original dry white Port from 1934, nutty and crisp and built for a Port and tonic. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Taylor's First Estate Reserve
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood for several years

taste.summary: An approachable reserve ruby with ripe berry fruit and a smooth, gently spiced finish.

pairings.why: An everyday Port for a mixed cheese board.
The reserve's spice frames the nutty sponge.

history.summary: Named for the Taylor's quintas, First Estate is the house's everyday reserve ruby.

milestones.event: House founded, the basis of the estate range

wines.description: Taylor's First Estate Reserve, an approachable everyday reserve ruby Port with ripe berry fruit. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged about two years in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: Fonseca's Vintage is the lush, exotically fruited counterpoint to Taylor's: opulent black fruit and a perfumed minty lift over firm structure.

pairings.why: Gamey richness meets the wine's concentrated dark fruit.
Salt and blue cheese against opulent sweet fruit.

history.summary: Fonseca has declared Vintage Port since the early 19th century, building a reputation for a richer, more exotic style than its Fladgate stablemate Taylor's.

milestones.event: Quinta do Panascal becomes the heart of the Vintage

wines.description: Fonseca Vintage Port, the lush exotically fruited classic of the Fladgate house, built around Quinta do Panascal. Best enjoyed 20 to 50 from vintage.

wines.name: Fonseca Bin 27
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood

taste.summary: One of the world's best-selling reserve rubies: rich, fruit-forward and smooth, a reliable everyday Port.

pairings.why: A versatile reserve for mixed cheeses.
Spice and dried fruit echo the wine.

history.summary: Fonseca created Bin 27 in 1972 as a premium reserve ruby; it became one of the category's best sellers.

wines.description: Fonseca Bin 27, one of the world's best-selling reserve ruby Ports, rich and fruit-forward. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca Late Bottled Vintage
wines.oak_regime: Aged four to six years in wood before bottling

taste.summary: A rich single-harvest LBV with the house's signature opulent black fruit, smooth and ready to drink.

pairings.why: Wood-aged sweetness against a sharp cheese.
Cocoa richness with the wine's black fruit.

history.summary: Fonseca's LBV bottles a single declared harvest aged longer in wood than Vintage, ready to enjoy on release.

wines.description: Fonseca Late Bottled Vintage, a rich single-harvest Port with the house's opulent black fruit. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine, mellow twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long candied-citrus finish.

pairings.why: Citrus and nuts echo the cask-aged profile.

history.summary: Aged tawnies sit alongside Fonseca's Vintage tradition, blended to a twenty-year average age.

wines.description: Fonseca 20 Year Old Tawny, a fine mellow cask-aged Port with honeyed dried fruit and a long finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca Terra Prima Organic Reserve
wines.oak_regime: Organically grown fruit, a reserve ruby aged in wood

taste.summary: An organic reserve ruby with ripe black fruit and a fresh herbal lift, made from certified organic vineyards.

history.summary: Terra Prima is Fonseca's organic reserve, drawn from certified organic Douro vineyards.

wines.description: Fonseca Terra Prima, an organic reserve ruby Port from certified organic Douro vineyards. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca Siroco Dry White Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in seasoned oak vats in a dry white style

taste.summary: A dry white Port, nutty and crisp, made for serving chilled or in a white Port and tonic.

pairings.why: Aperitif nuts with the nutty wine.

history.summary: Siroco is Fonseca's dry white Port aperitif style.

wines.description: Fonseca Siroco, a dry white Port made for serving chilled or as a white Port and tonic. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: The richest and sweetest of the classic Vintage Ports, built on the warm Malvedos and Tua fruit, with opulent black fruit over a firm spine.

pairings.why: Sweet black fruit cut by salty blue cheese.
Cocoa on the wine's liquorice and dark fruit.
Firm structure frames rich gamey meat.

history.summary: Graham's built its Vintage style on warm Cima Corgo and Douro Superior fruit, especially Quinta dos Malvedos, producing a richer and sweeter classic Vintage.

milestones.event: W and J Graham begin shipping Port

wines.description: Graham's Vintage Port, the richest and sweetest of the classic Vintages, built on warm Malvedos fruit. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's Six Grapes Reserve
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood

taste.summary: A premium reserve ruby evoking a young Vintage in miniature: concentrated fruit, smooth tannin and a warming finish.

history.summary: The Six Grapes name dates to the 19th century, marking casks of the finest reserve fruit; it remains Graham's flagship reserve ruby.

wines.description: Graham's Six Grapes Reserve, a premium reserve ruby evoking a young Vintage in miniature. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's Late Bottled Vintage
wines.oak_regime: Aged five years in wood before bottling

taste.summary: A rich single-harvest LBV aged five years in wood, smooth and approachable with dense black fruit.

history.summary: Graham's LBV bottles a single declared harvest aged five years in seasoned wood.

wines.description: Graham's Late Bottled Vintage, a rich single-harvest Port aged five years in wood. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's 10 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A nutty, honeyed ten-year tawny with dried-fruit sweetness and a clean finish.

pairings.why: Dried fruit lifts a rich cheese.

history.summary: Graham's aged tawnies draw on cask reserves at the 1890 Lodge.

milestones.event: 1890 Lodge becomes the ageing home for tawnies

wines.description: Graham's 10 Year Old Tawny, a nutty honeyed cask-aged Port with dried-fruit sweetness. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine, complex twenty-year tawny balancing honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long spiced finish, regularly among the top-rated of its class.

pairings.why: Dried fruit and nuts echo the wood ageing.

history.summary: The 20 Year Old is the heart of Graham's age-dated tawny range.

wines.description: Graham's 20 Year Old Tawny, a complex cask-aged Port balancing honeyed dried fruit, nuts and spice. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's 30 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average thirty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A concentrated thirty-year tawny with intense dried fruit, toffee and complex spiced wood.

pairings.why: Roasted coffee links wine and dessert.

history.summary: The 30 Year Old reflects decades of cask ageing in the Graham's lodge.

wines.description: Graham's 30 Year Old Tawny, a concentrated cask-aged Port with toffee, dried fruit and spice. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Graham's The Tawny
wines.oak_regime: A reserve tawny aged in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: An accessible reserve tawny with nutty caramel notes, made for everyday drinking chilled or neat.

history.summary: The Tawny is Graham's accessible reserve tawny.

wines.description: Graham's The Tawny, an accessible reserve tawny with nutty caramel notes for everyday drinking. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Dow's Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: The driest of the classic Vintage Ports, built around Quinta do Bomfim and Senhora da Ribeira fruit, with a firm structure and a notably dry, savoury finish.

pairings.why: The dry finish handles rare red meat.
Drier style cut by salty blue cheese.

history.summary: Dow's, owned by the Symingtons since the early 20th century, built its house style on a drier Vintage Port from Quinta do Bomfim and Senhora da Ribeira.

milestones.event: House origins as Silva and Cosens
Quinta do Bomfim becomes the heart of the Vintage

wines.description: Dow's Vintage Port, the driest of the classic Vintages, built around Quinta do Bomfim with a firm savoury finish. Best enjoyed 20 to 50 from vintage.

wines.name: Dow's Late Bottled Vintage
wines.oak_regime: Aged four to five years in wood before bottling

taste.summary: A drier-styled single-harvest LBV with black fruit and a savoury finish in the Dow's house style.

pairings.why: The drier finish suits red meat.

history.summary: Dow's LBV carries the house drier style into a single-harvest bottling.

wines.description: Dow's Late Bottled Vintage, a drier-styled single-harvest Port with black fruit and a savoury finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Dow's 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long spiced finish, slightly drier in the Dow's register.

pairings.why: Dried fruit and nuts echo the wood ageing.

history.summary: Dow's aged tawnies are blended at the Symington lodges to a twenty-year average age.

wines.description: Dow's 20 Year Old Tawny, a fine cask-aged Port with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Dow's Colheita
wines.oak_regime: A single-harvest tawny aged at length in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A single-harvest tawny matured for many years in cask, showing concentrated dried fruit and complex wood-aged spice from one declared year.

pairings.why: Roasted notes link wine and dessert.

history.summary: A Colheita is a tawny from a single harvest aged for years in wood before bottling; Dow's releases dated Colheitas from older reserves.

wines.description: Dow's Colheita, a single-harvest tawny aged at length in cask with concentrated dried fruit and wood spice. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Warre's Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: The most elegant and floral of the Symington Vintages, built on Quinta da Cavadinha fruit, with fine tannins and a fresh, perfumed character.

pairings.why: Elegant fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: The oldest British Port house, Warre's builds an elegant, floral Vintage style on Quinta da Cavadinha in the Pinhao valley.

milestones.event: Warre's founded, the oldest British Port house
Quinta da Cavadinha becomes the Vintage backbone

wines.description: Warre's Vintage Port, the most elegant and floral of the Symington Vintages, built on Quinta da Cavadinha. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks, bottled in a lighter style

taste.summary: A lighter, fresher ten-year tawny presented in a clear bottle, designed to be served chilled, with delicate dried-fruit and nut notes.

pairings.why: Delicate dried fruit lifts baked fruit.

history.summary: Warre's launched Otima in 1999 as a lighter, chilled-serve aged tawny in a distinctive clear bottle.

wines.description: Warre's Otima 10 Year Old Tawny, a lighter chilled-serve aged tawny in a distinctive clear bottle. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Warre's Warrior Reserve
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood

taste.summary: The world's oldest continuously shipped Port brand, a reliable reserve ruby with rich fruit and a smooth finish.

history.summary: Warrior is recognised as the oldest continuously shipped Port brand, dating to the mid-18th century.

wines.description: Warre's Warrior Reserve, the world's oldest continuously shipped Port brand, a rich reserve ruby. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Cockburn's Special Reserve
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood

taste.summary: One of the UK's best-loved reserve rubies, smooth and fruit-driven with a gently dry Cockburn's finish.

history.summary: Cockburn's Special Reserve became one of the best-selling reserve rubies in the UK market.

milestones.event: Special Reserve established as the flagship ruby

wines.description: Cockburn's Special Reserve, one of the UK's best-loved reserve ruby Ports, smooth and fruit-driven. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Cockburn's Fine Tawny
wines.oak_regime: A tawny aged in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: An accessible everyday tawny with nutty caramel notes, good chilled or neat.

history.summary: Fine Tawny is Cockburn's accessible everyday tawny.

wines.description: Cockburn's Fine Tawny, an accessible everyday tawny with nutty caramel notes. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Sandeman Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A structured Vintage Port from one of the most recognised Port houses, with concentrated black fruit and a firm spine for ageing.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Founded by George Sandeman in 1790, the house has declared Vintage Port for over two centuries, sourcing largely from Quinta do Seixo and Quinta do Vau.

wines.description: Sandeman Vintage Port, a structured Vintage from the famous caped-Don house, built for ageing. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Sandeman 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long candied-citrus finish.

history.summary: Sandeman aged tawnies are blended to a twenty-year average age at the Gaia lodges.

wines.description: Sandeman 20 Year Old Tawny, a fine cask-aged Port with honeyed dried fruit and a long finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Sandeman Founders Reserve
wines.oak_regime: A reserve ruby blend aged in wood

taste.summary: The house flagship reserve ruby, smooth and fruit-driven for everyday drinking.

history.summary: Founders Reserve is Sandeman's flagship reserve ruby.

wines.description: Sandeman Founders Reserve, the house flagship reserve ruby Port, smooth and fruit-driven. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Sandeman Apitiv White Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in seasoned oak vats in a white style

taste.summary: A white Port designed as a chilled aperitif, citrus-fresh and lightly nutty, ideal with tonic.

history.summary: Apitiv is Sandeman's white Port aperitif.

wines.description: Sandeman Apitiv White Port, a chilled aperitif white Port ideal with tonic. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal. Best enjoyed 0 to 2 from release.

wines.name: Barca Velha
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 18 months in new and used Portuguese and French oak

taste.summary: Portugal's most iconic dry red, made only in exceptional years from Douro Superior fruit: a structured, ageworthy wine with cedar, tobacco and a long mineral finish.

pairings.why: Firm tannins and herbs frame roast lamb.
Structure and acidity carry a rich sheep's cheese.
Gamey richness meets the wine's dark fruit and spice.

history.summary: Created by Fernando Nicolau de Almeida for Ferreira in 1952 after studying Bordeaux, Barca Velha proved the Douro could make a great dry red and is declared only in the finest vintages.

milestones.event: Most recent declarations continue the rare-release tradition

wines.description: Barca Velha, Portugal's most iconic dry red, made by Ferreira only in exceptional Douro Superior vintages. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Ferreira Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A structured Vintage Port from Portugal's most historic native house, with concentrated dark fruit and a firm ageing spine.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Ferreira has declared Vintage Port across its long history as Portugal's leading native-owned Port house.

wines.description: Ferreira Vintage Port, a structured Vintage from Portugal's most historic native-owned Port house. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Ferreira Dona Antonia Reserva
wines.oak_regime: A reserve tawny blend aged in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A reserve tawny named for the legendary Dona Antonia Ferreira, with nutty caramel and dried-fruit notes.

history.summary: Dona Antonia Reserva honours Dona Antonia Adelaide Ferreira, the 19th-century matriarch who shaped the house.

wines.description: Ferreira Dona Antonia Reserva, a reserve tawny named for the legendary Dona Antonia Ferreira. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Calem Velhotes Reserve Tawny
wines.oak_regime: A reserve tawny aged in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: Calem's well-loved everyday reserve tawny, nutty and lightly sweet, a staple of the Portuguese market.

history.summary: Velhotes is Calem's flagship everyday tawny, a familiar Portuguese brand.

wines.description: Calem Velhotes Reserve Tawny, the house's well-loved everyday tawny Port. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal. Best enjoyed 0 to 2 from release.

wines.name: Calem 10 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A nutty, honeyed ten-year tawny with dried-fruit sweetness and a clean cask-aged finish.

history.summary: Calem's age-dated tawnies are matured at its riverside Gaia cellars.

wines.description: Calem 10 Year Old Tawny, a nutty honeyed cask-aged Port with dried-fruit sweetness. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Kopke Colheita
wines.oak_regime: A single-harvest tawny aged for years to decades in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: The reference Colheita: a single-harvest tawny aged for many years in cask, intensely concentrated with dried fruit, nuts and candied citrus, released with its vintage on the label.

pairings.why: Caramelised custard meets the wine's candied citrus.

history.summary: Kopke, the oldest Port house, is the acknowledged master of Colheita, releasing single-harvest tawnies after decades in wood from one of the deepest cask reserves in the Douro.

milestones.event: Kopke founded, the oldest Port house

wines.description: Kopke Colheita, the reference single-harvest tawny from the oldest Port house, aged decades in cask. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Kopke 10 Year Old White Port
wines.oak_regime: A white Port aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A rare aged white Port, ten years in cask giving honeyed nutty depth and a gently oxidative, lingering finish.

pairings.why: Honeyed depth lifts a strong cheese.

history.summary: Kopke is one of the few houses to specialise in aged white Ports alongside its Colheitas.

wines.description: Kopke 10 Year Old White Port, a rare aged white Port with honeyed nutty depth. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal. A Douro Valley wine from Kopke.

wines.name: Burmester Colheita
wines.oak_regime: A single-harvest tawny aged for years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A single-harvest tawny in the Burmester house style, concentrated with dried fruit and nutty wood-aged complexity.

history.summary: Burmester is long known for its Colheita and aged tawny releases.

wines.description: Burmester Colheita, a concentrated single-harvest tawny in the house's tawny-led style. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Burmester 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long candied-citrus finish.

history.summary: Burmester blends its aged tawnies to a twenty-year average age.

wines.description: Burmester 20 Year Old Tawny, a fine cask-aged Port with honeyed dried fruit and a long finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A classic, fresh-styled Vintage Port from this artisan family house, with pure black fruit, fine tannins and a notably fresh, lifted structure.

pairings.why: Pure black fruit cut by blue cheese.
Fine fresh structure for gamey meat.

history.summary: Niepoort has declared Vintage Port across five generations of family ownership, favouring a fresher, more elegant classic style.

wines.description: Niepoort Vintage Port, a fresh, elegant classic Vintage from the artisan family house. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Colheita
wines.oak_regime: A single-harvest tawny aged for years to decades in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A celebrated single-harvest tawny, Niepoort holding some of the oldest Colheita reserves in the Douro, intensely concentrated with dried fruit and candied citrus.

history.summary: Niepoort is famous for its deep Colheita reserves, releasing single-harvest tawnies after long cask ageing.

wines.description: Niepoort Colheita, a celebrated single-harvest tawny from one of the deepest cask reserves in the Douro. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Redoma Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak, part new, for around 18 months

taste.summary: A pioneering elegant Douro red from old field-blend vines, fresh and mineral with red-fruit purity and savoury spice rather than weight.

pairings.why: Savoury spice and herbs frame the lamb.
Fresh acidity lifts the salt cod and potato.
Mineral structure carries a rich cheese.

history.summary: Dirk Niepoort launched Redoma in 1991 as one of the first modern, elegant Douro table wines, helping spark the region's still-wine renaissance.

milestones.event: First Redoma Tinto, an early modern Douro red

wines.description: Niepoort Redoma Tinto, a pioneering elegant old-vine Douro red, fresh and mineral with red-fruit purity. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Redoma Branco
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in old oak casks

taste.summary: A serious old-vine Douro white from field-blend vines, mineral and saline with a nutty cask-aged texture and racy acidity.

pairings.why: Texture and salinity carry the salt cod.
Crisp acidity cuts the fried cod cakes.

history.summary: Redoma Branco is one of the Douro's benchmark old-vine whites, from high-altitude field-blend vineyards.

wines.description: Niepoort Redoma Branco, a benchmark old-vine Douro white, mineral and saline with a nutty cask texture. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Batuta
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak, part new, for around 20 months

taste.summary: Niepoort's flagship old-vine Douro red, from very old field-blend vineyards: concentrated yet fresh, with graphite minerality and fine-grained structure for long ageing.

pairings.why: Structure and herbs frame the lamb.

history.summary: Batuta is Niepoort's top old-vine Douro red, sourced from ancient field-blend parcels and made for the cellar.

wines.description: Niepoort Batuta, the house's flagship old-vine Douro red, concentrated yet fresh with graphite minerality. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Charme
wines.oak_regime: Whole-bunch foot-trodden, aged in old oak in a Burgundian-inspired style

taste.summary: A deliberately Burgundian Douro red, whole-bunch and silky, prizing perfume and finesse over power, with fragrant red fruit and a savoury mineral finish.

pairings.why: Silky red fruit suits roast chicken or duck.

history.summary: Charme is Niepoort's whole-bunch, Burgundian-inspired Douro red, made for finesse.

wines.description: Niepoort Charme, a Burgundian-inspired whole-bunch Douro red prizing perfume and finesse. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Niepoort Dialogo Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged briefly in older oak to preserve fruit

taste.summary: Niepoort's fresh, fruit-forward entry Douro red, juicy and easy with soft tannins, an everyday introduction to the house style.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity lifts a red sauce.

history.summary: Dialogo is Niepoort's accessible everyday Douro range.

wines.description: Niepoort Dialogo Tinto, the house's fresh fruit-forward everyday Douro red. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal. Best enjoyed 1 to 6 from vintage.

wines.name: Real Companhia Velha Quinta de Cidro Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak for around 12 months

taste.summary: A modern, ripe Douro red from Royal Oporto's Quinta de Cidro, with dark berry fruit, rounded tannins and a fresh finish.

pairings.why: Ripe fruit and spice with lamb.

history.summary: Quinta de Cidro is one of Real Companhia Velha's flagship Douro estates for still wine.

wines.description: Real Companhia Velha Quinta de Cidro Reserva, a modern ripe Douro red from Royal Oporto. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Royal Oporto 10 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average ten years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A nutty, honeyed ten-year tawny under the Royal Oporto label from Portugal's oldest wine company.

history.summary: Royal Oporto is the long-standing Port brand of Real Companhia Velha.

wines.description: Royal Oporto 10 Year Old Tawny, a nutty honeyed tawny from Portugal's oldest wine company. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Noval Nacional
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then long bottle maturation

taste.summary: One of the rarest and most legendary wines in the world, from a tiny block of ungrafted, pre-phylloxera vines, declared only in exceptional years and capable of ageing for generations.

pairings.why: Only the greatest blue cheese matches its intensity.
A meditation wine to be contemplated on its own.

history.summary: Nacional comes from roughly two hectares of ungrafted vines on their own roots within the Noval estate, surviving phylloxera; declared only in the finest vintages, it is among the most sought-after Ports ever made.

milestones.event: Legendary 1931 Nacional vintage, a benchmark of the 20th century

wines.description: Quinta do Noval Nacional, from a tiny block of ungrafted pre-phylloxera vines, one of the rarest and most legendary Ports in the world.

wines.name: Quinta do Noval Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A pure, elegant single-quinta Vintage Port from the famous Pinhao estate, with fine tannins and graphite-edged black fruit built to age.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Quinta do Noval has declared Vintage Port from its single Pinhao estate for centuries; under AXA ownership the wines are prized for purity and elegance.

milestones.event: AXA Millesimes acquires Quinta do Noval

wines.description: Quinta do Noval Vintage Port, a pure elegant single-quinta Vintage from the famous Pinhao estate. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Noval Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged in French oak for around 14 months

taste.summary: A modern DOC Douro red from the Noval estate, with ripe dark fruit, fine tannins and the estate's signature freshness.

history.summary: Noval makes DOC Douro still wines alongside its Ports under AXA ownership.

wines.description: Quinta do Noval Tinto, a modern DOC Douro red from the famous Pinhao estate. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Noval 20 Year Old Tawny
wines.oak_regime: Aged on average twenty years in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: A fine twenty-year tawny with honeyed dried fruit, nuts and a long candied-citrus finish.

history.summary: Noval blends its aged tawnies to a twenty-year average age.

wines.description: Quinta do Noval 20 Year Old Tawny, a fine cask-aged Port with honeyed dried fruit and a long finish. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Croft Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A plush, approachably fruited Vintage Port from one of the oldest Port houses, built around the Quinta da Roeda estate.

pairings.why: Plush fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Croft, founded in 1588, builds its Vintage on Quinta da Roeda near Pinhao.

milestones.event: House founded, one of the oldest Port shippers

wines.description: Croft Vintage Port, a plush approachable Vintage from one of the oldest Port houses, built on Quinta da Roeda. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Croft Pink
wines.oak_regime: A rose Port, briefly aged and bottled fresh

taste.summary: The original rose Port: bright, fresh red-berry fruit designed to be served chilled over ice or in a cocktail.

pairings.why: The signature chilled rose Port serve.

history.summary: Croft launched Pink in 2008 as the first commercial rose Port, creating a new category.

milestones.event: Croft Pink launches the rose Port category

wines.description: Croft Pink, the original rose Port, bright fresh red-berry fruit served chilled over ice. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Crasto Vinha Maria Teresa
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden, aged around 18 months in French oak, part new

taste.summary: A single-vineyard old-vine field blend from a plot of pre-1920 vines, dense and structured yet fresh, with graphite minerality and a long savoury finish built for the cellar.

pairings.why: Structure and herbs frame the lamb.

history.summary: Vinha Maria Teresa is Crasto's flagship single-vineyard wine, from a small plot of mixed old vines planted before 1920, named for the estate matriarch.

wines.description: Quinta do Crasto Vinha Maria Teresa, a flagship single-vineyard old-vine field blend built for the cellar. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Crasto Reserva Old Vines
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 14 to 16 months in French oak, part new

taste.summary: A benchmark old-vine field-blend Douro red, ripe yet structured, balancing dark fruit and savoury spice with the estate's signature freshness and ageability.

history.summary: The Reserva Old Vines, from mixed vineyards over 70 years old, helped establish the modern Douro red category.

wines.description: Quinta do Crasto Reserva Old Vines, a benchmark old-vine field-blend Douro red. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Crasto Superior
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: A polished Douro Superior red from the estate's Vale de Cabrões vineyard, with ripe dark fruit, rounded tannins and freshness.

history.summary: Crasto Superior comes from the estate's Douro Superior vineyard at Vale de Cabroes.

wines.description: Quinta do Crasto Superior, a polished Douro Superior red with ripe dark fruit and freshness. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Crasto Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from the Crasto estate, fresh and structured with concentrated black fruit.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Crasto declares a single-quinta Vintage Port in the best years alongside its still wines.

milestones.event: Modern Crasto Vintage Port declarations begin

wines.description: Quinta do Crasto Vintage Port, a fresh structured single-quinta Vintage from the Crasto estate. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Vale Meao Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 18 months in French oak, part new

taste.summary: One of Portugal's most sought-after dry reds, from the historic Douro Superior estate where Barca Velha was born: powerful yet refined, with cedar, graphite and a long structured finish.

pairings.why: Structure and herbs frame the lamb.

history.summary: After the Ferreira-linked family regained the Vale Meao estate, Francisco Olazabal launched the eponymous wine in 1999; the estate was the original home of Barca Velha.

milestones.event: Estate created by Dona Antonia Ferreira
First Quinta do Vale Meao Tinto under family label

wines.description: Quinta do Vale Meao Tinto, one of Portugal's most sought-after dry reds, from the historic estate where Barca Velha was born. Best enjoyed 8 to 30 from vintage.

wines.name: Meandro do Vale Meao
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: The second wine of Vale Meao, ripe and polished with the estate's signature freshness, an accessible introduction to the house style.

history.summary: Meandro is the second wine of Quinta do Vale Meao, made from younger vines and declassified lots.

wines.description: Meandro do Vale Meao, the polished second wine of the famous Douro Superior estate. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Chryseia
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak, mostly new

taste.summary: A refined, Bordeaux-influenced Douro red from the Symington and Prats partnership: silky and polished, prizing elegance and freshness over power.

pairings.why: Polished tannins and herbs with lamb.

history.summary: Chryseia, from the 1998 partnership of the Symingtons and Bordeaux's Bruno Prats, debuted in 2000 to bring Bordeaux finesse to Douro fruit.

wines.description: Chryseia, a refined Bordeaux-influenced Douro red from the Symington and Prats partnership. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Post Scriptum de Chryseia
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: The second wine of Chryseia, fresh and elegant with red-and-black fruit, an accessible window onto the house's Bordeaux-influenced style.

pairings.why: Fresh red fruit suits roast chicken.

history.summary: Post Scriptum is the second wine of Chryseia.

wines.description: Post Scriptum de Chryseia, the fresh elegant second wine of Chryseia. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal. Best enjoyed 3 to 12 from vintage.

wines.name: Quinta do Vesuvio Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in granite lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A powerful single-quinta Vintage Port from the grand Douro Superior estate, foot-trodden in granite lagares, with dense black fruit and a mineral, ageworthy structure.

pairings.why: Dense black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Bought by the Symingtons in 1989, Vesuvio makes a single-quinta Vintage Port every declared year, still trodden by foot in its granite lagares.

wines.description: Quinta do Vesuvio Vintage Port, a powerful single-quinta Vintage from the grand Douro Superior estate, foot-trodden in granite lagares.

wines.name: Quinta do Vallado Adelaide
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 18 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Vallado's flagship old-vine Douro red, named for Dona Antonia Adelaide Ferreira: concentrated and structured yet fresh, from low-yielding old vines.

history.summary: Adelaide is Vallado's top cuvee, honouring the estate's link to Dona Antonia Adelaide Ferreira.

wines.description: Quinta do Vallado Adelaide, the estate's flagship old-vine Douro red, concentrated yet fresh. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Vallado Reserva Field Blend
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: An old-vine field-blend Reserva, ripe and structured with savoury spice and the estate's hallmark freshness.

history.summary: The Reserva Field Blend comes from Vallado's old mixed-variety vineyards.

wines.description: Quinta do Vallado Reserva Field Blend, an old-vine Douro red with savoury spice and freshness. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Wine & Soul Pintas
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden, aged around 18 months in French oak, part new

taste.summary: The wine that launched Wine & Soul, from a tiny plot of ancient field-blend vines at Vale de Mendiz: dense and mineral yet fresh, a benchmark of the modern Douro.

pairings.why: Structure and herbs frame the lamb.

history.summary: Sandra Tavares da Silva and Jorge Serodio Borges made the first Pintas in 2001 from an old mixed-variety plot, launching their acclaimed project.

milestones.event: First Pintas vintage launches Wine & Soul

wines.description: Wine & Soul Pintas, the dense mineral old-vine field blend that launched the acclaimed Vale de Mendiz project. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Wine & Soul Guru
wines.oak_regime: Fermented and aged in French oak

taste.summary: A serious old-vine Douro white, mineral and saline with a textured cask-aged palate and racy acidity, one of the region's reference whites.

pairings.why: Crisp acidity cuts the fried cod.

history.summary: Guru is Wine & Soul's old-vine field-blend white, a benchmark Douro white.

wines.description: Wine & Soul Guru, a serious old-vine Douro white, mineral and saline with racy acidity. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Wine & Soul Manoella Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: An approachable Douro red from the Manoella estate, ripe and fresh with fine tannins, the everyday wine of Wine & Soul.

pairings.why: Fresh acidity lifts a red sauce.

history.summary: Manoella is the family estate range, the accessible Wine & Soul wines.

wines.description: Wine & Soul Manoella Tinto, an approachable ripe Douro red, the everyday wine of the estate. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Wine & Soul 5G Tawny Port
wines.oak_regime: A long-aged tawny drawn from old family cask reserves

taste.summary: A rare old tawny from five generations of cask reserves (5G), intensely concentrated with dried fruit and wood-aged complexity.

history.summary: 5G is Wine & Soul's old tawny, drawn from cask reserves passed down five generations of the Borges family.

wines.description: Wine & Soul 5G Tawny Port, a rare old tawny from five generations of family cask reserves. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta Nova Grande Reserva
wines.producer_name: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 16 months in French oak, part new

taste.summary: A concentrated, oak-aged Cima Corgo red from the Amorim estate, structured and fresh with dark fruit and cedar spice.

history.summary: Grande Reserva is the flagship red of Quinta Nova, a pioneer of Douro wine tourism.

wines.description: Quinta Nova Grande Reserva, a concentrated oak-aged Cima Corgo red from the Amorim estate. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta Nova Referencia Grande Reserva
wines.producer_name: Quinta Nova de Nossa Senhora do Carmo
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 18 months in new French oak

taste.summary: Quinta Nova's top old-vine red, dense and mineral with cedar and graphite, made only in the best years.

history.summary: Referencia is Quinta Nova's prestige old-vine red.

wines.description: Quinta Nova Referencia Grande Reserva, the estate's top old-vine red, dense and mineral. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Portal Grande Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 14 to 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: An oak-aged Pinhao valley red, ripe and structured with dark fruit and cedar, from the architect-designed Portal estate.

history.summary: Grande Reserva is the flagship red of Quinta do Portal near Sabrosa.

wines.description: Quinta do Portal Grande Reserva, an oak-aged Pinhao valley red, ripe and structured. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Portal Moscatel do Douro
wines.oak_regime: Fortified and aged in seasoned oak casks in a sweet Moscatel style

taste.summary: A rare fortified Moscatel do Douro, intensely aromatic with orange blossom and candied citrus, sweet but lifted by fresh acidity.

pairings.why: Sweet wine cuts salty blue cheese.

history.summary: Portal makes a rare aged fortified Moscatel from the Favaios-area Moscatel Galego grape.

wines.description: Quinta do Portal Moscatel do Douro, a rare fortified Moscatel, intensely aromatic with orange blossom. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta de la Rosa Douro Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak

taste.summary: A polished single-quinta Douro red from the riverside estate below Pinhao, ripe and fresh with fine tannins.

history.summary: La Rosa was an early mover in modern Douro still wine, made at its riverside quinta below Pinhao.

milestones.event: First estate-bottled La Rosa Douro red

wines.description: Quinta de la Rosa Douro Tinto, a polished single-quinta Douro red from the riverside estate below Pinhao. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta de la Rosa Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A fresh single-quinta Vintage Port from the riverside La Rosa estate, with black fruit and a firm structure.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: La Rosa makes a single-quinta Vintage Port alongside its still wines.

milestones.event: First estate La Rosa Vintage Port

wines.description: Quinta de la Rosa Vintage Port, a fresh single-quinta Vintage from the riverside estate below Pinhao. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta da Pacheca Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 to 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: A polished south-bank Douro red from the historic Pacheca estate near Lamego, ripe and rounded with dark fruit and spice.

history.summary: Pacheca, one of the first Douro quintas to bottle under its own name, makes a flagship Reserva red.

wines.description: Quinta da Pacheca Reserva, a polished south-bank Douro red from the historic estate near Lamego. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta da Pacheca Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from the Pacheca estate, with black fruit and a firm structure for ageing.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Pacheca makes a single-quinta Vintage Port alongside its still wines.

wines.description: Quinta da Pacheca Vintage Port, a single-quinta Vintage from the historic south-bank estate. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Tedo Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured, organically grown fruit

taste.summary: An organic single-quinta Vintage Port from the Tedo-Douro confluence, with black fruit and a firm ageing structure.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Quinta do Tedo farms organically and makes a single-quinta Vintage Port at the confluence of the Tedo and Douro.

wines.description: Quinta do Tedo Vintage Port, an organic single-quinta Vintage from the Tedo-Douro confluence. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Tedo Finineza Douro Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 12 months in French oak, organically grown fruit

taste.summary: An organic Douro red from the Tedo estate, ripe and fresh with fine tannins and dark fruit.

history.summary: Tedo makes organic DOC Douro reds alongside its Port.

wines.description: Quinta do Tedo Finineza Douro Tinto, an organic Douro red, ripe and fresh from the Tedo estate. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Pego Grande Reserva
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 16 months in French oak

taste.summary: An award-winning oak-aged red from the Pego estate above the river at Valenca do Douro, concentrated and structured with dark fruit and cedar.

history.summary: Grande Reserva is the flagship red of the Pego wine hotel and estate.

milestones.event: Estate founded as a wine hotel and producer

wines.description: Quinta do Pego Grande Reserva, an award-winning oak-aged red from the estate above Valenca do Douro. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quevedo Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A well-priced single-quinta Vintage Port from this family producer at Sao Joao da Pesqueira, with black fruit and a firm structure.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: The Quevedo family makes approachable, well-priced Vintage Port from their Douro Superior vineyards.

milestones.event: Family begins bottling under its own name

wines.description: Quevedo Vintage Port, a well-priced single-quinta Vintage from the family producer at Sao Joao da Pesqueira. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quevedo Tawny Reserva
wines.oak_regime: A reserve tawny aged in seasoned oak casks

taste.summary: An accessible reserve tawny with nutty caramel notes, good chilled or neat.

history.summary: Quevedo's reserve tawny is an everyday cask-aged Port.

wines.description: Quevedo Tawny Reserva, an accessible everyday cask-aged Port with nutty caramel notes. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Vallegre Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from the Vista Alegre estate near Pinhao, with black fruit and a firm structure.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Vallegre makes Vintage Port from its Quinta da Vista Alegre near Pinhao.

wines.description: Vallegre Vintage Port, a single-quinta Vintage from the Vista Alegre estate near Pinhao. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta da Gaivosa Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 16 to 18 months in French oak

taste.summary: The flagship old-vine red from the Gaivosa estate in the cooler Baixo Corgo, structured and mineral with a long fresh finish.

history.summary: Gaivosa is the flagship red of Alves de Sousa, a Baixo Corgo family pioneer of Douro still wine.

wines.description: Quinta da Gaivosa Tinto, the flagship old-vine red from the Alves de Sousa estate in the cooler Baixo Corgo. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Alves de Sousa Abandonado
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 18 months in new French oak

taste.summary: A prestigious old-vine red from a once-abandoned terraced vineyard, dense and mineral with graphite and savoury spice, built for long ageing.

history.summary: Abandonado comes from old vines on a terraced plot that had been left abandoned, recovered by the family.

wines.description: Alves de Sousa Abandonado, a prestigious old-vine red from a recovered abandoned terraced vineyard. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta da Romaneira Tinto
wines.oak_regime: Aged around 14 months in French oak

taste.summary: A polished estate red from the large historic Romaneira property between Pinhao and Alijo, ripe and fresh with fine tannins and cedar spice.

history.summary: Romaneira makes DOC Douro reds alongside its luxury hotel on one of the region's most scenic stretches of river.

wines.description: Quinta da Romaneira Tinto, a polished estate red from the large historic property between Pinhao and Alijo. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta da Avessada Moscatel de Favaios
wines.oak_regime: Fortified and aged in seasoned oak casks in the sweet Moscatel de Favaios style

taste.summary: The signature sweet fortified Moscatel of the Favaios plateau, intensely aromatic with orange blossom, candied citrus and honey, lifted by fresh acidity.

pairings.why: Sweet wine cuts salty blue cheese.

history.summary: Moscatel de Favaios is a distinctive sweet fortified wine of the Favaios plateau; Avessada is one of its leading ambassadors through its wine museum and tastings.

milestones.event: Estate origins on the Favaios plateau

wines.description: Quinta da Avessada Moscatel de Favaios, the signature sweet fortified Moscatel of the Favaios plateau. DOC Douro, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Quinta do Seixo Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from Sandeman's flagship Cima Corgo estate at Valenca do Douro, concentrated and structured for ageing.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Quinta do Seixo is Sandeman's modern Cima Corgo estate, source of a single-quinta Vintage Port.

milestones.event: Sandeman develops the modern Seixo winery

wines.description: Quinta do Seixo Vintage Port, a single-quinta Vintage from Sandeman's flagship Cima Corgo estate. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Dow's Quinta do Bomfim Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from the Symington home estate at Pinhao, made in years Dow's does not declare a classic Vintage, in the drier Dow's house style.

pairings.why: The dry finish suits red meat.
Drier style cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Quinta do Bomfim is the Symington family home quinta and the heart of Dow's; its single-quinta Vintage is declared in off years.

milestones.event: Bomfim becomes central to Dow's Vintage

wines.description: Dow's Quinta do Bomfim Vintage Port, a single-quinta Vintage from the Symington home estate at Pinhao. DOC Porto, from the Douro Valley of northern Portugal.

wines.name: Fonseca Quinta do Panascal Vintage Port
wines.oak_regime: Foot-trodden in lagares, aged in vat then bottle-matured

taste.summary: A single-quinta Vintage Port from Fonseca's source estate in the Tavora valley, declared in off years, with the house's opulent black fruit and minty lift.

pairings.why: Black fruit cut by blue cheese.

history.summary: Quinta do Panascal is Fonseca's source estate; its single-quinta Vintage is declared in years Fonseca does not declare a classic Vintage.

milestones.event: Panascal becomes the heart of Fonseca Vintage

wines.description: Fonseca Quinta do Panascal Vintage Port, a single-quinta Vintage from Fonseca's source estate in the Tavora valley. Best enjoyed 15 to 35 from vintage.
